name: Oreo Cookie Tube Cake
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------------CHOCOLATE GLAZE-------------------------------
6 ounces Semi-sweet chocolate chips
1 tablespoon Milk
3 tablespoons Shortening
1 tablespoon Corn syrup
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour a 9-inch fluted tube pan. Cut each cookie into quarters. In a large bowl, with the mixer on low speed, beat together the flour and remaining ingredients except for the cookies and chocolate glaze until blended. Increase the speed to high and beat for 2 minutes, scraping the bowl. Spoon about 3/4 cup batter into the pan. Gently stir in the cut-up cookies and spoon them into the same pan. Bake for 50 minutes or until the cake springs back when lightly touched with a finger (the toothpick test won't work). Cool the cake in the pan on a wire rack for 10 minutes, then remove from the pan and cool on a rack. When the cake is cooled, prepare the chocolate glaze. In a quart saucepan over low heat, heat the chocolate chips, shortening, milk, and corn syrup until melted and smooth. Place the rack with the cake on a large plate to catch drips, then pour the warm glaze over the top and sides of the cake. This cake freezes well and is wonderful cold from the refrigerator.
